title: Oil pulls back to $104.50 late on Pakistan talks hope — WTI similar pattern
snippet: Oil prices pull back from $107.38 highs to $104.50 in late trading on renewed hope for Pakistan talks. Witkoff/Kushner departure Saturday and Araghchi's Islamabad arrival seen as positive signals. WTI drops from $98 high to $95.20. Analysts say $2-3 'diplomacy discount' could persist if talks materialize. Still up ~16% for the week.
tag_reason: Late-day oil pullback on talks hope is new price action. Shows market sensitivity to diplomatic signals. $2-3 'diplomacy discount' concept is new.
